title: T-062 — restore proper org-scoped USING clause on user_pins RLS (FT-002)
prompt:
# T-062 — restore proper org-scoped USING clause on user_pins RLS (FT-002)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x, §8.x, §13
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-002** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P1** (S effort)

## Goal
Replace the temporary USING (true) policy on user_pins with the canonical org-scoped policy and add cross-org SQLSTATE 42501 integration tests.

## Implementation contract
1. New migration `025-user-pins-rls-restore.sql` that DROPs the temporary policy and recreates `user_pins_org_isolation` with USING + WITH CHECK both org-scoped via app.current_org_id().
2. Schema TypeScript stays import-only; no API surface changes.
3. Integration test exercises both: (a) same-org user can SELECT their pin; (b) cross-org user gets SQLSTATE 42501 (insufficient_privilege).

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/migrations/025-user-pins-rls-restore.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/user-pins-rls.integration.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/schema/user-pins.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 025 applied, when the policy on user_pins is inspected, then USING and WITH CHECK both reference the org-scoped subquery (no USING (true)).
2. Given an app-role connection scoped to org A, when SELECT runs against a user_pins row owned by org B, then the row count is 0 and no error leaks the existence of the row.
3. Given an app-role connection scoped to org A, when INSERT/UPDATE attempts to write a user_pins row whose user belongs to org B, then SQLSTATE 42501 is raised.

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/db/__tests__/user-pins-rls.integration.test.ts asserting cross-org isolation before migration 025 exists; tests must fail.
- Pin SQLSTATE 42501 for cross-org denial.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test user-pins-rls`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not keep USING (true) anywhere on user_pins.
- Do not use raw `new pg.Pool` — use app-role connection helper.
- Do not skip pinning SQLSTATE — bare rejects.toThrow() is forbidden per T-055 lint rule.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Restore proper org-scoped USING clause on user_pins RLS that was relaxed to USING (true) during T-016 emergency fix; verify cross-org isolation.
pipeline_inputs.details: During T-016 verify-PIN landing, user_pins RLS USING clause was temporarily relaxed to USING (true) WITH CHECK (false) to unblock the verify path. This task restores the proper USING (user_id IN (SELECT id FROM users WHERE org_id = app.current_org_id())) USING+WITH CHECK pair and proves cross-org isolation with a pinned-SQLSTATE integration test.
out_of_scope: Do not touch any other RLS policy in this task.

Given an app-role connection scoped to org A, when INSERT/UPDATE attempts to write a user_pins row whose user belongs to org B, then SQLSTATE 42501 is raised.
